skillName: Substituting into expressions with one operation
misconceptionSummary: Students can perform substitution mechanically but do not see it as replacing an unknown with a known value.

visuals.ruleText: Substitution means replacing a variable with a number
visuals.subText: The letter is a placeholder for a value

steps.narration: Substitution means replacing a letter with the number it stands for.
Once the value is known, the letter no longer stays mysterious. It is replaced by that number everywhere it appears.
This matters especially with multiplication. Two p means two times p, so after substitution it becomes ordinary multiplication.

lines.text: One operation means one calculation after substitution

steps.narration: In one-operation substitution, once the value has been inserted, there is just one arithmetic step to do.

visuals.label: Substitution turns an algebraic expression into arithmetic

steps.narration: That is the core idea. Substitution turns algebra into arithmetic by replacing letters with known values.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: treating the letter and number as separate symbols instead of replacement. If p = 6, then p is no longer unknown in that question. It stands for 6.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Do not keep the letter once its value has been given. Replace it with the number and then work with the arithmetic expression.
